title: relbunium hypocarpium subsp. fluminense
intro: Relbunium hypocarpium subsp. fluminense, also known as Relbunium or Brazilian Relbunium, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, native to Brazil. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Relbunium hypocarpium subsp. fluminense include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and flu. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in herbal remedies, such as tinctures and extracts, to harness its medicinal properties.
